things.name: Dom Tower (Domtoren)
things.description: The tallest church tower in the Netherlands at 112 metres; climb 465 steps to panoramic views over Utrecht and the surrounding flatlands

things.name: Canals and Wharves
things.description: Utrecht's unique two-level canal system with 12th-century wharf cellars now housing restaurants and cafes; the most atmospheric waterway experience in the Netherlands

things.name: Centraal Museum
things.description: Oldest city museum in the Netherlands with art from the 17th century to the present; includes the world's largest collection of Rietveld furniture and Utrecht School paintings

things.name: Rietveld Schroder House (UNESCO)
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage icon of De Stijl architecture designed by Gerrit Rietveld in 1924; a revolutionary home where every wall and floor can be repositioned

things.name: Museum Speelklok
things.description: Unique museum of self-playing musical instruments from street organs to fairground carousels; every 45 minutes instruments burst into life in a joyful demonstration

things.name: Miffy Museum (Nijntje Museum)
things.description: Enchanting museum dedicated to Miffy, the beloved bunny character created by Dick Bruna who was born in Utrecht; a magical experience for young children

things.name: Utrecht Old Town
things.description: One of the Netherlands' best-preserved medieval city centres with the grand Oudegracht canal, Roman-era street plan and richly decorated guild houses

things.name: Sonnenborgh Museum and Observatory
things.description: 19th-century observatory in a 16th-century bastion of the city fortifications; guides explain the history of astronomy and weather observation in the Netherlands

things.name: Griftpark
things.description: Modern urban park on the former Grift industrial site with a canoe course, playground and outdoor events; a popular spot for Utrecht's young, vibrant population

things.name: Galgenwaard Stadium
things.description: Home of FC Utrecht with 24,000 capacity; the club competed in the UEFA Europa League group stage in 2017-18 and are a regular Dutch top-four side
